provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content:
1. Short title
This Act may be cited as the Botswana National Sport Commission Act.

provisions.title: Interpretation

provisions.chapter: PART II Establishment and Functions of the Commission (ss3-4)
provisions.title: Continuation of establishment of Commission
provisions.content:
3. Continuation of establishment of Commission
(1) The Council known as the Botswana National Sports Council, established under the repealed Act, is hereby continued under the new name of the Botswana National Sport Commission.
(2) The Commission shall be a body corporate with perpetual succession and a common seal, capable of suing and being sued in its own name and, subject to the provisions of this Act, of performing such acts as bodies corporate may by law perform.

provisions.chapter: PART II Establishment and Functions of the Commission (ss3-4)
provisions.title: Functions of the Commission

provisions.chapter: PART III Administration of the Commission
provisions.title: Establishment of Board

definitions.definition: a body registered under the Societies Act (Cap. 18:01), and duly recognised by the International Olympic Committee as the national level structure for dealing with Olympic matters
the Chairperson of the Board appointed under section 5(2)(a)
the Chief Executive Officer of the Commission appointed under section 20(1)
the Botswana National Sport and Commission established under section 3
the Disciplinary Committee appointed under section 27
a team officially representing the country
a body registered under the Societies Act, and duly recognised by the International Paralympic Committee as the national level structure for dealing with Paralympic matters
a body registered under the Societies Act, which has as its principal objective the promotion or organisation of any sport or recreation activity at national level
the Botswana National Sports Council Act (Cap. 60:01) repealed under section 44
a body registered under the Societies Act, and duly recognised by the International Working Group on Women and Sport as the national level structure for dealing with women in sport.
